text.title: Alert-to-Runbook Resolution Rule
text.summary: The agent loads runbooks by deterministic path lookup and refuses to act on unmatched alerts.

section.title: Alert name resolves to one runbook path
section.p: The alert's `runbook_url` annotation (Prometheus / PagerDuty / Datadog) MUST point to a file inside `runbooks/`. The agent fetches the file at incident creation, validates the spine, and refuses to execute any remediation when the file is missing, untagged, or fails the schema check.

rule.statement: The agent MUST resolve the runbook path from the alert's `runbook_url` annotation, MUST verify the file contains all four canonical sections, and MUST refuse all remediation (returning `runbook_missing` to the orchestrator) when the lookup fails. The agent MUST NOT invent or "search" for a runbook.
rule.rationale: Lookup-by-search lets the agent pick a wrong-but-plausible runbook from a similarly named service, which is how cross-service blast radius incidents start. A failed lookup is a feature: it pages the human to author the missing runbook.

example.code: # Prometheus alert - alert: HighDBLatency annotations: runbook_url: "github://faionfaion/ops/runbooks/HighDBLatency.md" summary: "p99 DB latency > 500ms for 5m"
example.note: Path is explicit, repo-versioned, code-reviewed. Agent validates spine before parsing steps.

example.code: # Anti: agent searches for "closest" runbook by alert name runbook = vector_search(alert.name, top_k=1) agent.execute(runbook.remediate_steps)
example.note: Vector match silently picks a wrong runbook for a similarly named service; remediation hits the wrong cluster.

antipattern.description: Falling back to a "default runbook" when the lookup misses.
antipattern.reason: A default runbook for arbitrary alerts cannot encode service-specific safety; "restart the service" applied to a stateful primary is a data-loss event.

section.reference: Reference parser used by the agent loader.
